questions.question: What is the primary goal of demand paging in virtual memory management?
questions.explanation: Demand paging aims to reduce the number of page faults by loading pages into memory only when they are needed.

questions.question: Which of the following techniques is used to modify data in a page while it is being paged out to disk?
questions.explanation: Copy-on-write is a technique that allows data to be modified in a page while it is being paged out to disk, by creating a copy of the page before making any changes.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using a page table instead of a frame table in virtual memory management?
options: D) Better tracking of page faults
questions.explanation: A page table is more flexible and scalable than a frame table, making it a better choice for virtual memory management.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a valid page table entry?
options: A) It must contain a valid frame number
B) It must contain a valid page number
C) It must contain both a valid frame number and a valid page number
D) It must contain a valid reference bit
questions.explanation: A valid page table entry must contain both a valid frame number and a valid page number.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a page directory in a virtual memory management system?
options: A) To translate virtual addresses to physical addresses
questions.explanation: A page directory is used to translate virtual addresses to physical addresses, and it contains pointers to the page tables for each page in memory.
